name: Melat Tekie
title: Full-Stack Developer, Frontend Engineer
bio: Developer, designer, data person; in that order depending on the day. I like building things that actually get used.

experience.role: B.S. Computer Science, Minor in Psychology
experience.narrative: I studied Computer Science with a minor in Psychology at Minnesota State University Moorhead, graduating Magna Cum Laude with a 3.69 GPA and making the Dean's List seven out of eight semesters. Outside the classroom, I mentored fourth graders in Scratch programming as a 'code sensei' and supervised a team of thirteen student workers on campus construction projects — early lessons in both teaching and leading.

My first role out of school was building solutions on the Salesforce CRM platform using Apex and JavaScript. I learned the real rhythms of professional development here — daily standups, sprint planning, and managing support tickets through Jira.

I stretched into new territory here — developing iOS apps in Objective-C and Xcode, integrating an e-commerce site with Klaviyo through PHP, and building on Dynamics 365 CRM with C#, Java, and jQuery. I also connected ConnectWise with other ticketing systems via its SDK, my first real taste of systems integration.

Contracting for Microsoft, I analyzed quarterly reports and built Macros to automate recurring processes. Working with SQL, SAS, Python, and Power BI on large datasets taught me how to turn raw numbers into decisions people could actually act on.

experience.role: M.S. Business Analytics & Graduate Research Associate II
experience.narrative: While earning my Master's, I worked as a Graduate Research Associate — maintaining Sitecore CMS components, building Power BI and Python reporting dashboards, and optimizing site performance and SEO. I was honored with the Wayne Watts Fellowship for this work.

Today, I sit between stakeholders and engineering — building custom SharePoint web parts, running QA on live applications, designing UX prototypes in Figma, and writing governance standards for Power Platform development. It's the role where design, data, and development finally come together.

projects.name: Catering Platform
projects.description: A local restaurant needed a way to take catering orders online instead of relying on phone calls and guesswork. I built a full ordering system in Next.js with a live Supabase backend — customers browse the real menu, use a headcount calculator to see pricing update in real time, and submit inquiries that land directly in the owner's inbox. The result: a self-serve ordering flow that didn't exist before, cutting out manual back-and-forth for every quote.

projects.name: Portfolio Engine
projects.description: Building a portfolio for every future client from scratch would mean rewriting the same page over and over. Instead, I designed a single Astro-based engine driven entirely by JSON — each person's name, bio, skills, and projects live in their own data file, and one dynamic route renders any of them on demand. Adding a new client's portfolio now means duplicating a template and filling in their info, not building a new site from zero.
